title: For-loop conditions should be true at least once
description: For-loop conditions should be true at least onceAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugWhy is this an issue?More InfoAforloop is a fundamental programming construct used to execute a block of code repeatedly. However, if the loop’s condition is false before the first iteration, the loop will never execute.for (int i = 0; i < 0; i++) // Noncompliant: the condition is always false, the loop will never execute { // ... }Rewrite the loop to ensure the condition evaluates totrueat least once.for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++) // Compliant: the condition is true at least once, the loop will execute { // ..
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects for loops where the initial condition is provably false (e.g., i = 0; i < 0 or i = 10; i < 5)
logic.triggering_conditions: Triggers when: ForStatement contains hardcoded initial value and comparison that is false from the start
logic.exclusion_conditions: Excludes: loops with variable conditions, comments, test code
logic.reasoning: For loops should execute at least once to be meaningful. This rule detects obvious cases where hardcoded numeric values make the loop condition false from the start, such as 'i = 0; i < 0' or 'i = 10; i < 5'. Pattern matches for loop initialization with literal integers followed by comparison operators with another literal that makes the condition impossible. Excludes variable-based conditions since those may be dynamic.
